title: The One That Could Have Been (Part 1)

jokes.text: So it seems like this Internet thing is here to stay, huh?
jokes.setup: Character making a casual observation about technology
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about the permanence of the Internet
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with good economy, playing on the Internet's obvious permanence by 2000

jokes.text: It's okay not to talk.
jokes.setup: Awkward silence after Ross's Internet comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry acknowledgment of the conversational failure
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deadpan with excellent economy and character voice

jokes.text: No. Barry and Mindy. Oh, sorry. I hear 'divorce,' I immediately go to Ross.
jokes.setup: Monica needs to clarify which divorce she's talking about
jokes.punchline: Ross's multiple divorces are so notorious they're her default reference
jokes.explanation: Strong character-based humor that builds on established series mythology

jokes.text: But that just means he was falling asleep on top of her instead of me.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Barry cheating on Rachel with Mindy
jokes.punchline: Rachel's twisted logic about the nature of Barry's infidelity
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy, subverts expectations about infidelity

jokes.text: Which time? -The first time!
jokes.setup: Ross wondering about life without divorce
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate clarification needed due to multiple divorces
jokes.explanation: Good callback execution with snappy timing

jokes.text: I can't. I keep seeing it the good way.
jokes.setup: Ross asking Chandler to imagine if Carol hadn't realized she was lesbian
jokes.punchline: Chandler can only imagine the sexual scenario, not the relationship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect Chandler voice and economy. Subverts the emotional question into something purely sexual.

jokes.text: Maybe the problem was you were pronouncing it 'ka-ra-tay.'
jokes.setup: Ross explaining his karate hobby during marriage troubles
jokes.punchline: Chandler mocks Ross's pronunciation as the reason for ineffectiveness
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay joke with good character voice, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: You wouldn't be dating me, that's for sure. -Sure I would. -Come on. You guys really think I'm that shallow? No, I just think Monica was that fat.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if Chandler would date her if she was still fat
jokes.punchline: Chandler's brutal honesty about Monica's former size
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with brutal honesty that's both shocking and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Hey. Hey, there's Carol again.
jokes.setup: Joey mentions getting fired from Days of Our Lives
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately thinks of Ross's lesbian ex-wife scenario
jokes.explanation: Good callback with economy, though impact is moderate

jokes.text: But my job's fun too. I mean, tomorrow... I don't have to wear a tie.
jokes.setup: Ross talking about writing for The New Yorker
jokes.punchline: Chandler's pathetic attempt to make his corporate job sound exciting
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor showing Chandler's job dissatisfaction

jokes.text: Because at that time, I thought that everything that rhymed was true. I thought if I worked with stocks... I'd have to live in a box and only eat lox and have a pet fox.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why she didn't take a Wall Street job
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre rhyming logic about the consequences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor perfectly suited to Phoebe's character. Original concept with excellent wordplay and structure.

jokes.text: Yeah, you don't want that in a broker.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's absurd rhyming logic about working in stocks
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry understatement about the unsuitability
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction to Phoebe's absurdity with excellent economy

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. Ross Tilman. -No. No, no. Ross Geller. -Of course! Monica's brother.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to reintroduce himself to Rachel
jokes.punchline: The awkward confusion about his name
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward interaction, functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: Busty Ladies? No, that's okay. Some kid asked me to pick it up for him, but I...
jokes.setup: Ross apparently buying pornography
jokes.punchline: His embarrassed excuse about buying it for someone else
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe humor with Ross's character embarrassment

jokes.text: Here you go, buddy. Sorry, no porn for you.
jokes.setup: Ross's excuse about buying for someone else
jokes.punchline: Him actually following through on the excuse
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the setup with Ross's earnestness

jokes.text: You know, the hazelnut, actually not a nut. It's a seed. -Wow. Can anyone else name a well-known seed that's been masquerading as a nut? -Oh, dear God. Let me think.
jokes.setup: Richard sharing boring trivia about hazelnuts
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic enthusiasm for more trivia
jokes.explanation: Strong character comedy establishing Richard's boring nature and Chandler's sarcastic reaction

jokes.text: They said my writing was funny, just not 'Archie Comic' funny.
jokes.setup: Chandler got another rejection letter
jokes.punchline: The specific type of humor rejection
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor about specific comedy standards

jokes.text: By the way, the answer is: the Brazil nut. -Was his question, 'What's more boring than him?'
jokes.setup: Richard providing the answer to his trivia question
jokes.punchline: Chandler's cutting insult about Richard's boringness
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback with sharp wit and perfect timing

jokes.text: I may have no money, but I still have my pride. -Really?
jokes.setup: Chandler declaring he has his pride despite being broke
jokes.punchline: Joey's simple questioning of this claim
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of Chandler's dignity

jokes.text: Since I don't need any assistance in there, take a break!
jokes.setup: Joey hiring Chandler as his assistant
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal interpretation of when assistance is needed
jokes.explanation: Decent character humor showing Joey's simplemindedness

jokes.text: No. No, no. I said sell when it hits 50! Five-oh! It's a number! It comes after four-nine!
jokes.setup: Phoebe on a business call as a stockbroker
jokes.punchline: Her condescending explanation of basic numbers
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Phoebe's unique approach to business

jokes.text: No, it's okay. It's okay. You're allowed one mistake. Just kidding. You are, of course, fired.
jokes.setup: Phoebe seemingly being understanding about the mistake
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal to firing the person
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with perfect timing and character voice

jokes.text: Did you lose weight? -You are so sweet to notice. Yes, I lost 3.5 pounds!
jokes.setup: Rachel complimenting Monica on weight loss
jokes.punchline: Monica's overly specific and minimal weight loss
jokes.explanation: Good character humor about Monica's precision with weight

jokes.text: It's hard to be friends with Drake... because of his busy schedule and the fact that he's not real.
jokes.setup: Rachel excited about being friends with Dr. Drake Ramoray
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact explanation about the character's non-existence
jokes.explanation: Sharp deadpan humor with excellent economy and timing

jokes.text: What's your name? What kind of name is 'Brindy'? Stop talking. All right, from now on your name is Joan. You can pick your own last name.
jokes.setup: Phoebe interviewing a potential employee
jokes.punchline: Her arbitrary renaming of the person
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor showing Phoebe's bizarre management style

jokes.text: I can't find my pen. Did you eat that too?
jokes.setup: Phoebe looking for her pen
jokes.punchline: Accusing someone of eating it along with other things
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Phoebe's unique logic

jokes.text: I'm delivering twins today, but only one of them is mine.
jokes.setup: Ross talking about his work day
jokes.punchline: Clarification that reveals he's an obstetrician delivering someone else's twins
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection with good economy and medical context

jokes.text: 'Teach me how to spell vitamins.' 'Wear in my new jeans.' -You realize what you are, don't you? -What? You're his bitch!
jokes.setup: Chandler reading Joey's ridiculous assistant tasks
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt assessment of the power dynamic
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation with Monica's direct, harsh assessment

jokes.text: Wait! You didn't sit on my Kit Kats did you? No, there was a little, little dip in the market... and I lost 13 million dollars. But the Kit Kats are all right?
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking about Kit Kats after losing millions
jokes.punchline: Her priorities being completely backwards
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Phoebe's skewed priorities in a crisis

jokes.text: If I were, would I have shooting pains up and down my left arm? -Yes. -Then, yes. That is what I'm having.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if she's having a heart attack
jokes.punchline: Her logical but delayed realization
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with Phoebe's delayed understanding

jokes.text: Honey, having a heart attack is nature's way of telling you to slow down. -I always thought having a heart attack was nature's way of telling you to die.
jokes.setup: Monica's optimistic take on heart attacks
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's much darker interpretation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor subverting the typical optimistic platitude

jokes.text: You're not gonna die. I mean, you are going to die, but you're not gonna die today.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to reassure Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Her accidentally morbid clarification
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Monica's well-intentioned but poorly executed comfort

jokes.text: You should consider writing for Talking Out of Your Ass magazine.
jokes.setup: Someone giving Phoebe unwanted advice
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's creative insult about their advice quality
jokes.explanation: Creative insult with good wordplay and Phoebe's voice

jokes.text: How long has it been since you've had sex? -Well, last weekend... -Oh, that's not so bad. will be two months... since I stopped trying.
jokes.setup: Ross seemingly admitting to recent sexual activity
jokes.punchline: The much more depressing reality of his situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection structure with perfect timing and Ross's pathetic situation

jokes.text: Or you could be two stockbrokers, and you're rolling naked on the trading floor... and everybody's watching. -It never happened.
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting sexual role-play scenarios
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's immediate denial about her stockbroker fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Phoebe's defensive reaction

jokes.text: One is: 'Bring me food.' Two is: 'I'm with a girl. Bring us food.' Three is: 'I'm lost and I can't find food.'
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining Joey's beeper codes
jokes.punchline: All codes revolving around food
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Joey's one-track mind about food

jokes.text: Go take off those pants. They look ready.
jokes.setup: Chandler wearing in Joey's new jeans
jokes.punchline: Joey's assessment of the jeans being properly worn in
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Joey's simple logic about clothing readiness

jokes.text: And that is a large piece of television equipment. And that is an old man. -Hey, old man! -Hey.
jokes.setup: Joey giving Rachel a tour of the TV set
jokes.punchline: His casual interaction with random crew member
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor with Joey's casual rudeness

jokes.text: I may have said those things before, but... I never truly meant them until now. -That's a line from the show too!
jokes.setup: Joey claiming his romantic lines are sincere
jokes.punchline: Rachel catching him using another scripted line
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Joey's reliance on scripted material

jokes.text: No pulp. Pulp isn't juice. All juice.
jokes.setup: Joey complaining about pulp in orange juice
jokes.punchline: His emphatic declaration about juice purity
jokes.explanation: Decent character humor showing Joey's strong preferences

jokes.text: You're gonna throw that juice at me, aren't you? -It's not all juice.
jokes.setup: Joey anticipating Chandler's revenge
jokes.punchline: Chandler's technical correction before the attack
jokes.explanation: Good setup and payoff with Chandler's pedantic correction

jokes.text: We could have a threesome. -I love that idea!
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting various ways to spice up their sex life
jokes.punchline: Carol's immediate enthusiasm for the threesome idea
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that sets up Carol's lesbian revelation later

jokes.text: My last Kit Kat bar.
jokes.setup: Chandler wanting to give something special to celebrate
jokes.punchline: His pathetic offering of candy
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing Chandler's limited resources

jokes.text: You know how Monica feels about low-fat mayonnaise? It's not mayonnaise!
jokes.setup: Joey comparing his hatred of pulp to Monica's food opinions
jokes.punchline: His emphatic declaration about mayonnaise authenticity
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting character quirks about food authenticity

jokes.text: As his butler. And then makes him do crazy things... Like bring him milk shakes that can't have lumps in them.
jokes.setup: Chandler describing his Archie Comics story
jokes.punchline: The story mirrors his own experience with Joey
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor where Chandler unknowingly writes about his own life

jokes.text: I just cannot believe this. I mean, Joey Tribbiani. -You don't have to use his last name. But, Monica, come on, it's Joey Tribbiani. -I guess you have to.
jokes.setup: Rachel's excitement about Joey using his full name
jokes.punchline: Monica's resigned acceptance that the full name is necessary
jokes.explanation: Good escalation about celebrity name usage

jokes.text: I wish we could just not be married for a little bit. You know, I just wish we could be, like, on a break.
jokes.setup: Rachel wishing she wasn't married
jokes.punchline: Her use of the famous 'on a break' phrase
jokes.explanation: Strong callback to series mythology, though somewhat expected

jokes.text: Yeah, I can. Don't think I don't. I do. I mean, all the time. You bet!
jokes.setup: Rachel saying Monica can have sex with whoever she wants
jokes.punchline: Monica's increasingly desperate overcompensation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's insecurity about her virginity

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You're a 30-year-old virgin. -Say it louder. The guy in the back didn't hear you. Yeah, I heard it.
jokes.setup: Rachel loudly announcing Monica's virginity
jokes.punchline: The random guy confirming he heard it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with perfect timing from the background character

jokes.text: What do you know, virgin?
jokes.setup: Monica giving Rachel advice about cheating
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dismissive insult using Monica's virginity
jokes.explanation: Decent callback insult with good economy but somewhat mean-spirited

jokes.text: The doctor said that could be one of the side effects. -Put the cigarette out. No, it's not a cigarette. The smoke is coming out of me!
jokes.setup: Smoke coming from the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Phoebe claiming smoke is emanating from her body
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Phoebe's literal interpretation of side effects

jokes.text: Yeah, she's fired.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking if her work was mad about her absence
jokes.punchline: Monica's casual delivery of the bad news
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery with misdirection structure

jokes.text: Yeah, sure, sure, I can bring some sandwiches.
jokes.setup: Rachel calling Joey about their date
jokes.punchline: Her misunderstanding what kind of encounter this is
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Rachel's innocence about the situation

jokes.text: Why do you always wear that dumb hat? What, you late for your paper route? 'Hi, Mr. and Mrs. Jones. Here's your paper. I'm sorry about your window.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe insulting someone's hat
jokes.punchline: Her elaborate paper boy scenario with property damage
jokes.explanation: Creative insult with good escalation and Phoebe's unique voice

jokes.text: People are not supposed to have heart attacks at 31. -I know. But if I didn't work there, what else would I do? -You used to like playing the guitar. -That was lucrative. Smart like your brother.
jokes.setup: Monica suggesting Phoebe return to guitar playing
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic response about the financial prospects
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm with Phoebe's realistic assessment of artistic careers

jokes.text: But if you think you could put a fish in there... and it wouldn't get sucked into the mechanism, you'd be wrong.
jokes.setup: Joey showing off his apartment's water feature
jokes.punchline: His casual mention of fish death in the fountain
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good specificity and Joey's casual delivery

jokes.text: They have parasites?
jokes.setup: Joey mentioning what people say about oysters
jokes.punchline: Rachel's completely unsexy interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Rachel's innocence derailing Joey's seduction

jokes.text: All right, relax, Mr. I've-Had-Sex-Four-Times! -Four different women! I've had sex way more times. -How many? -Nine.
jokes.setup: Monica mocking Chandler's sexual experience
jokes.punchline: Her own pathetically low number despite the boast
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with Monica's competitive nature leading to embarrassment

jokes.text: I was kidding. -So was I.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if Chandler wants to have sex with her
jokes.punchline: Both claiming they were kidding in an uncomfortable moment
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment with both characters backpedaling

jokes.text: Do you have any moves? -I have some moves. I have no moves.
jokes.setup: Monica asking about Chandler's sexual expertise
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate honesty after brief bravado
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Chandler's typical insecurity

jokes.text: Oh, my God! I know! I'll tell you something. We are gonna do that again!
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler after their first sexual encounter
jokes.punchline: Monica's enthusiastic declaration about repeating it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's competitive/enthusiastic nature about sex

jokes.text: Not after seeing that.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if they had sex after she threw up
jokes.punchline: Joey's honest assessment of the mood killer
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Joey's blunt honesty

jokes.text: I'm not a 'star.' I'm just a regular famous actor.
jokes.setup: Rachel calling Joey a TV star
jokes.punchline: Joey's distinction between star and famous actor
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic perfectly suited to Joey's character

jokes.text: But I thought that ring stood for Capri's undying love for her brother. -Look, you want the ring or not?
jokes.setup: Joey trying to give the ring meaningful symbolism
jokes.punchline: His impatient abandonment of the romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Joey's practical approach to romance

jokes.text: Look, Ross, I think Carol's great, and I'm sure you're very attractive, but...
jokes.setup: Ross asking Joey about threesomes
jokes.punchline: Joey's assumption that Ross is propositioning him
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Joey's misunderstanding creating awkwardness

jokes.text: Made a snack. -What'd you have? -Just a sandwich. Turkey. A little mustard. -Sounds good. -It really was.
jokes.setup: Ross getting bored during his threesome
jokes.punchline: His casual sandwich making and detailed description
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor subverting expectations about threesomes with mundane detail

jokes.text: Hey, check me out. I'm a slut!
jokes.setup: Monica having had sex with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Her proud declaration of promiscuity after one encounter
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Monica's enthusiasm and naivety

jokes.text: But after being with you, I'm like, 'Can the doctor see me now?' -I bet he can. 'I don't have an appointment, but I sure could use a physical.'
jokes.setup: Monica feeling confident after sex with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Their back-and-forth sexual innuendo about medical appointments
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo with good back-and-forth structure

jokes.text: Phoebe, you don't have an office. -I'll work right here. This is good. Next to this plant.
jokes.setup: Phoebe claiming she'll be in her office after being fired
jokes.punchline: Her immediate adaptation to working by a plant
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with Phoebe's delusional persistence

jokes.text: She's gay. Oh, my God. She is so gay!
jokes.setup: Discussion about Ross's wife engaging with another woman
jokes.punchline: Ross's delayed realization about Carol's sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Ross finally understanding the obvious

jokes.text: I'm the ruptured spleen.
jokes.setup: Monica wondering who beeped Dr. Roger
jokes.punchline: Chandler's confession of medical impersonation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with excellent economy and Chandler's desperate measures

jokes.text: There was just one woman, wasn't there? -No, there were two. -Including me? -Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Monica asking about Chandler's sexual experience
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Monica herself was one of only two women
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing Chandler's limited experience

jokes.text: And that is just what I will do Soon as those bastards I worked for Die
jokes.setup: Phoebe singing about saying goodbye to hate and anger
jokes.punchline: Her vengeful conclusion about her former employers
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor subverting the peaceful song with violent wishes

tmdb_overview: Barry and Mindy's divorce prompts fantasies. Alternate versions: Ross married to Carol, Rachel to Barry (fawning over Joey), Monica overweight, Chandler writing, Phoebe a Wall Street executive.
